info.name: joe-404/laravel-auth
info.description:
Complete API collection for the joe-404/laravel-auth Laravel library.

**Two ready-to-use folders:**
- **SPA — Session & Cookie** — for browser/SPA clients. Uses Laravel session cookies + CSRF token. No Authorization header needed on authenticated requests. A folder-level pre-request script automatically fetches `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` before every request in this folder.
- **API — Bearer Token** — for mobile apps and API clients. Uses `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` on every authenticated request. Sends `X-Client-Type: mobile` on login to get a token.

**Quick start (SPA mode):**
1. Set `base_url` variable to your app URL
2. Run **Setup → Get CSRF Cookie** once
3. Call **SPA / Registration / 1. Initiate Registration**, verify email, then **Login**
4. Session cookie is stored automatically by Postman's cookie jar

**Quick start (API/mobile mode):**
1. Set `base_url` variable
2. Call **API / Registration / 1. Initiate Registration**, verify email, then **Login**
3. `auth_token` and `refresh_token` are saved automatically
4. Use `POST /auth/token/refresh` before token expires

**Variables:**
- `base_url` — your Laravel app URL (e.g. `http://localhost:8000`)
- `auth_token` — auto-saved on API login / verify / Google OAuth
- `refresh_token` — auto-saved on API login / verify / token refresh
- `xsrf_token` — auto-saved by SPA folder pre-request script
- `temp_token` — auto-saved after registration initiation
- `reset_token` — auto-saved after magic link password reset step
- `api_token_id` — auto-saved after creating an API token
- `api_token_raw` — auto-saved after creating an API token
- `session_id` — auto-saved after listing sessions

item.name: Setup
item.description: Run these once before using the SPA folder.

request.description:
**Run this before making any SPA/web mode requests.**

Sanctum sets two cookies:
- `laravel_session` — your session identifier
- `XSRF-TOKEN` — the CSRF token

The test script saves the token to `xsrf_token`. The SPA folder's pre-request script also does this automatically before every request.

**Not required for the API / Bearer Token folder.**

item.name: SPA — Session & Cookie
item.description:
All requests configured for browser/SPA clients.

**How it works:**
- A folder-level pre-request script fetches `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` automatically before every request and saves the token to `xsrf_token`.
- All mutating requests (POST/DELETE/PATCH) include `X-XSRF-TOKEN: {{xsrf_token}}`.
- Authenticated requests use the session cookie set by Postman's cookie jar — no Authorization header needed.
- Login returns `token: null`; Sanctum sets the session cookie on the response.

**Required:** `AUTH_MODE=web` or `AUTH_MODE=both` (without `AUTH_SPA_TOKEN=true`).

exec: pm.sendRequest({ url: baseUrl + '/sanctum/csrf-cookie', method: 'GET' }, function(err, res) {

request.description:
Initiates registration. Sends OTP + magic link to the email (one combined email when `AUTH_VERIFICATION_METHOD=both`).

Does **not** create the user yet — that happens on verification.

Auto-saves `temp_token` for Reverb channel subscription.

**Extra fields example** — if your app requires more data, add them here:
```json
{
  "email": "joe@example.com",
  "password": "Password123!",
  "password_confirmation": "Password123!",
  "phone": "+96170000000",
  "birthday": "1990-01-01"
}
```

**Errors:** 409 already registered · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

exec: console.log('SPA mode — session cookie set, no token returned.');

request.description:
Completes registration using the OTP from the email. Replace `123456` with the actual code.

In SPA mode the response `token` is `null` — Sanctum sets the session cookie.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid or expired

Paste the full magic link URL from the verification email. The library validates the signature and completes registration.

When `AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_TARGET=frontend`, your frontend receives `?token=uuid` and calls this endpoint with the token.

**Errors:** 422 invalid/expired signature or token already used
Resends the verification OTP/link for a pending (unverified) registration. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

exec: console.log('SPA mode — session cookie set by Sanctum. No token returned.');

request.description:
Logs in and receives a session cookie. The response `token` is `null` — Sanctum sets `laravel_session` and `XSRF-TOKEN` cookies that Postman stores automatically.

**Important:** This request intentionally has no `Accept: application/json` header. Sending that header can trigger token mode in `AUTH_MODE=both`. Remove it so the server sees a browser-like request.

**Errors:** 401 wrong credentials/locked · 403 inactive/unverified · 422 validation · 429 rate limit
Returns the authenticated user's profile, roles, permissions, and active session count. Authenticated via session cookie — no Authorization header needed.
Revokes the current session only. The session cookie is invalidated.
Revokes ALL sessions and tokens for this user across every device.
Sends OTP and/or magic link for password reset. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

Resets the password in one step using the OTP from the email. All existing sessions are revoked on success.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid/expired

Paste the full URL from the password reset email. Validates the signature and returns a `reset_token` valid for 15 minutes.

Auto-saves `reset_token` to collection variables. Then call **2c. Confirm Reset**.

Submits the new password with the `reset_token` from step 2b. All sessions revoked on success.

**Errors:** 422 reset_token invalid or expired (15 min window)

Changes password for the currently authenticated user.

**`logout_all: true`** — revokes all other sessions except the current one.

**Errors:** 422 current_password incorrect or validation failed
Lists all active sessions with device, IP, geo location, and last-active time. Auto-saves a non-current `session_id` for the delete request.
Revokes the session with the given ID. Also revokes the associated Sanctum token if it was a token-based session.

Lists all third-party API tokens owned by the authenticated user.

**Requires:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

Creates a new third-party API token. The `raw_token` in the response is shown **only once** — store it securely.

**`abilities`:** array of strings. Use `["*"]` for full access.
**`expires_in_days`:** `null` = never expires. Max 3650.

Token format: `auth_at_{base64}`

Auto-saves `api_token_raw` and `api_token_id`.
Revokes the API token with the given ID. Uses `api_token_id` saved by the Create request.

Returns the Google OAuth authorization URL. Copy `data.redirect_url` and open it in a browser to start the OAuth flow.

**Requires:** `AUTH_GOOGLE_ENABLED=true`

**Errors:** 403 Google OAuth disabled

Google redirects the user's browser here after authorization. In practice you do not call this directly — Google's redirect handles it.

**Handles 3 cases automatically:** existing provider link · email match · new user.

**Errors:** 403 disabled or inactive · 400 OAuth exchange failed

item.name: API — Bearer Token
item.description:
All requests configured for mobile apps and API clients.

**How it works:**
- Login sends `X-Client-Type: mobile` to receive a Bearer token even in `AUTH_MODE=both`.
- Every authenticated request includes `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}`.
- No CSRF cookie or session cookie needed.
- A `refresh_token` is issued alongside the access token — use **Token Refresh** to rotate them before expiry.

**Required:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

request.description:
Initiates registration. Sends OTP + magic link to the email.

Does **not** create the user yet — that happens on verification.

Auto-saves `temp_token` for Reverb channel subscription.

**Extra fields example:**
```json
{
  "email": "joe@example.com",
  "password": "Password123!",
  "password_confirmation": "Password123!",
  "phone": "+96170000000",
  "birthday": "1990-01-01"
}
```

**Errors:** 409 already registered · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

Completes registration. Returns `token` (access) and `refresh_token`. Both are auto-saved.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid or expired

Paste the full magic link URL from the verification email. Returns `token` and `refresh_token`, both auto-saved.

**Errors:** 422 invalid/expired or already used
Resends OTP/magic link for a pending registration. Always returns 200.

header.description: Optional — enables detailed device tracking in auth_sessions_extended

request.description:
Logs in and returns a Bearer `token` (access) + `refresh_token`. Both are auto-saved to collection variables.

`X-Client-Type: mobile` forces token mode in `AUTH_MODE=both`.

Enable `X-Device-Info` to track device model, platform, and OS version in the session record.

**Errors:** 401 wrong credentials/locked · 403 inactive/unverified · 422 validation · 429 rate limit
Returns the authenticated user's profile, roles, permissions, and active session count.
Revokes the current Bearer token and its associated session record.
Revokes ALL tokens and sessions for this user across every device.

Exchanges the current `refresh_token` for a new access token + refresh token pair. Both old tokens are revoked immediately.

Call this before the access token expires to keep the user logged in without re-entering credentials. The new tokens are auto-saved to `auth_token` and `refresh_token`.

Token lifetimes are configured per client type in `config/auth_system.php` under `token_ttl`.

**Errors:** 401 refresh token invalid, expired, or already used · 429 rate limit
Sends OTP and/or magic link for password reset. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

Resets the password in one step using the OTP from the email. All existing tokens and sessions are revoked on success.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid or expired
Paste the full URL from the password reset email. Returns a `reset_token` valid for 15 minutes. Auto-saved to collection variables.

Submits the new password with the `reset_token` from step 2b. All tokens and sessions revoked on success.

**Errors:** 422 reset_token invalid or expired (15 min window)

Changes password for the currently authenticated user.

**`logout_all: true`** — revokes all other tokens except the current one.

**Errors:** 422 current_password incorrect or validation failed
Lists all active sessions with device, IP, geo location, and last-active time. Auto-saves a non-current `session_id` for the delete request.
Revokes the session with the given ID and its associated Sanctum token.

Lists all third-party API tokens owned by the authenticated user.

**Requires:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

Creates a new third-party API token. The `raw_token` is shown **only once** — store it securely.

**`abilities`:** array of strings. Use `["*"]` for full access.
**`expires_in_days`:** `null` = never expires. Max 3650.

Token format: `auth_at_{base64}`

Auto-saves `api_token_raw` and `api_token_id`.
Revokes the API token saved in `api_token_id`.

item.name: Use API Token on a Protected Route

request.description:
Example of calling a route protected by `auth.api-token` middleware. Replace the URL with your actual endpoint.

The middleware validates:
1. Token starts with `auth_at_`
2. SHA-256 hash matches a stored record
3. Token is active and not expired
4. Token has the required abilities

Returns the Google OAuth authorization URL. Open `data.redirect_url` in a browser to start the OAuth flow.

**Requires:** `AUTH_GOOGLE_ENABLED=true`

**Errors:** 403 Google OAuth disabled

exec: console.log('auth_token saved from Google OAuth:', body.data.token);

request.description:
Google redirects the user's browser here after authorization. In practice you do not call this directly.

**Handles 3 cases automatically:** existing provider link · email match · new user.

Auto-saves `auth_token` and `refresh_token`.

**Errors:** 403 disabled or inactive · 400 OAuth exchange failed

item.name: Admin — API Tokens
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing API tokens across all users.

**Requires:** `super-admin` or `admin` role.

Use `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` (logged in as an admin user).

request.description: Lists all API tokens across all users with owner info, abilities, expiry, and last-used timestamp.
Creates an API token not attached to any user — useful for service accounts or CI pipelines.

Updates abilities and/or expiry of any API token.

**Fields (all optional):**
- `abilities` — replaces the current abilities array
- `expires_in_days` — `null` clears expiry (never expires)
Revokes any API token regardless of owner.

item.name: Reference
item.description: Response format examples and HTTP status code reference.

request.description:
Every error response follows this shape:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Human readable error.",
  "errors": { }
}
```

**HTTP status codes used:**
- `200` OK
- `201` Created
- `400` Bad Request
- `401` Unauthorized
- `403` Forbidden
- `404` Not Found
- `409` Conflict (email already registered)
- `422` Unprocessable Entity (validation)
- `423` Locked (account lockout)
- `429` Too Many Requests (rate limit)
